text: The laparoscopic view shows the left fallopian tube and ovary attached to the posterior uterus. The surgeon uses four ports: a 10mm port at the umbilicus, a 10mm port at the left inguinal region, and two 5mm ports at the right inguinal region and suprapubic area. The left pelvic brim is visible as the surgeon inspects for the ureter, ensuring no adhesions are present, and begins dissecting the left tube from the ovary and bowel.

The video shows the left and right ovaries and tubes attached to the posterior aspect of the uterus. The left ovary proprium is visible, and the right ovary and tube are also adhered to each other. An endometrial implant is present in the surgical field.

The surgeon dissects the right pararectal space starting from the right pelvic brim, identifying and carefully separating the right ureter from the peritoneum. A chocolate cyst is visible on the right ovary, and the right tube is being dissected from the ovary. On the left side, the surgeon continues to dissect the ureter from adhesions involving the ovary and bowel, while also working to separate the ovary from the rectum.
The ovary is visibly adhered to the rectosigmoid colon. The peritoneum is exposed, and the parorectal space is identified between the ureter and the rectum.

The surgeon observes severe adhesions with the ovary attached to the rectus sigmoid. The peritoneum is visible, and the left ureter is being carefully dissected from the peritoneum, progressing towards the uterosacral ligament insertion on the uterus.

The surgeon uses a rectal probe to aid in the dissection of the rectovaginal space, which is obscured due to adhesions between the ureter, uterus sacral ligament, and rectosigmoid. Bimanual examination is performed to differentiate the vagina from the rectum, confirming the identification of the rectovaginal space. The surgeon checks for nodules and observes adhesions between the rectosigmoid and the pelvic wall.

The surgeon holds and dissects the fibrotic right utero-sacral ligament from the rectum. The recto-vaginal space is mobilized, revealing the right and left utero-sacral ligaments, the right tube, right ovary, and the right ureter. The left ovary, left ureter, and left utero-sacral ligament are also visible after adhesiolysis.
